symptoms.remedy: Point guard penetrates without help; kick-out passes produce wide-open 3-pointers. Correction: corner defenders must be up near the elbows.
symptoms.symptom: Corner defenders stay in the corner

symptoms.remedy: Quick slip pass over the top for an easy catch-and-score. Correction: stay one or two steps above your man.
One side is 3-on-2 or 4-on-3. Correction: the four off-ball defenders must all shift to cover elbows and boxes on the ball side.
Drive to the lane; no help available. Correction: force sideline; never let the ball go middle in this set.
X4 must beat Player 4 to the spot; playing behind creates an easy low-post feed.
symptoms.symptom: X4 losing Player 4 under the cross screen

symptoms.remedy: A late rotation leaves the paint open; X5 must move as the trap forms, not when the ball is released.
symptoms.symptom: X5 failing to pre-rotate before the pass

symptoms.remedy: After helping on Player 3's penetration, X2 must sprint back to Player 2 on the weak side or risk an open three.
If X4 follows the roll when Player 5 actually pops, the open three-pointer is gifted; read the roller's body language immediately.
gives the offensive player a step-up or cut option; always face the ball
symptoms.symptom: Playing on top of the screen recipient with back to ball

symptoms.remedy: overrun = screening your own teammate
symptoms.symptom: Screener's defender chasing the screener after he cuts

symptoms.remedy: gives the screen recipient a clean path to his spot
cutter receives ball with space to drive or shoot; ball defender must drop to nail immediately
symptoms.symptom: Ball defender not pressuring after the pass

symptoms.remedy: the handoff screen is a trap for defenders who lose their positioning; must go through/inside
symptoms.symptom: Getting caught in the screen on a DHO

symptoms.remedy: the trap must occur at the moment of the handoff — late trapping lets the receiver get a step before help arrives
when trapping, the passer can slip away for a backdoor or dive to the basket; weak-side help must account for this
symptoms.symptom: Not being aware of the slip

symptoms.remedy: receiver gets a free catch-and-shoot; correction: communicate early and go through inside
backdoor cut goes uncovered; correction: third defender stays alert to the cutter
symptoms.symptom: Both defenders following the ball on the switch

symptoms.remedy: gives the passer a running start on the return cut; correction: step back only enough to allow the through-go
symptoms.symptom: Defender retreating too much on get action

symptoms.remedy: The lob over the front goes unchecked for an easy layup; X5 must anticipate and close before the pass.
symptoms.symptom: X5 failing to pre-rotate when X1 fronts

symptoms.remedy: X3/X4/X2 must maintain awareness of both the ball and their assignments in the help rotation.
If the opponent's best player catches cleanly at the low post, the defense is in trouble; fronting is preferable for elite scorers.
symptoms.symptom: X1 going behind on a known scorer

symptoms.remedy: Easy catch at the elbow opens up easy scores for a skilled big. Correction: overplay and contest all flash-post entries.
symptoms.symptom: Post defender doesn't deny the flash

symptoms.remedy: Wing slips backdoor for an easy layup. Correction: *"keep his man in front of him if possible"* — always maintain man awareness.
symptoms.symptom: Wing defender watches the ball instead of man

symptoms.remedy: Backdoor cutter gets an easy layup off the flash. Correction: the low-block defender MUST cross the lane on any backdoor attempt.
Quick pass to the weak side corner produces open shots. Correction: nail, elbow, box must be occupied immediately.
Offense gets a layup. Correction: Low-block defender has an absolute assignment to cross the lane on flash-post action. [S1, p.148]
Passer finds a cutter or open perimeter player. Correction: Step back, widen, and control passing lanes rather than lunging at ball handler. [S1, p.148]
symptoms.symptom: Post defender attacks the ball instead of passing lanes

symptoms.remedy: Gut pass reaches the middle. Correction: Open up, retreat to goal, or snap back rather than losing floor position. [S1, p.148]
symptoms.symptom: Wing defender chases his man and vacates passing lane

symptoms.remedy: screens your own teammate; correction: bump, step out, hold up
gives him cutting/step-up options; correction: always move toward the ball
symptoms.symptom: Playing on top of down-screen receiver with back to the ball

symptoms.remedy: fader catches open; correction: recognize the fade early and shoot the gap
cutter's defender has no help; correction: screener's defender must show on every screen
cutters can use screens at full speed; correction: make contact with the screener before he is set
Passer's defender gets screened off and cutter receives an easy layup pass. Correction: post defender must step back and create a clear lane before the cutter arrives.
Ball handler gets a clean pull-up jumper at the elbow. Correction: fight over all pinch-post screens.
Post player dribbles into the paint unopposed. Correction: nail, elbow, and box must be filled immediately on any pinch-post action.
Middle defender leaves his zone and the pass splits the defense. Correction: force all passes over the outside shoulder of the trapper.
One cutter goes free; the switch is split. Correction: Back defender must call it early and loudly — "screen, switch!" [S1, p.149]
Quick passes exploit depth disadvantage. Correction: Both defenders must adjust upward on the pass to the post so they are already in position when the split begins. [S1, p.149]
symptoms.symptom: Defenders not level with the ball

symptoms.remedy: Offensive player dribbles through the middle of the switch. Correction: Both defenders must come together aggressively and not allow any splitting of the seam. [S1, p.149]
stay level with the ball, use peripheral vision, ball-you-man principle
symptoms.symptom: Losing sight of the ball while guarding a weak-side cutter

symptoms.remedy: screener's defender must never overrun and pick off their own teammate
soft defense allows easy low-block catches and cuts to the goal
symptoms.symptom: Failing to body up on screen recipients

symptoms.remedy: every play in this system requires maximum ball pressure to succeed
three-quartering gives the lock-and-lob opportunity
symptoms.symptom: Post defender not fronting when ball is at or below FT line

symptoms.remedy: level with ball must be the default, not sagging too deep
symptoms.symptom: Weak-side defenders not being drawn to the ball

symptoms.remedy: cutter shortcuts under both screens for an easy catch; must trail first and shortcut second
symptoms.symptom: Going over the first screen too aggressively

symptoms.remedy: cutter gets clean cuts; screeners must be held up
symptoms.symptom: Screener's defenders not standing up screeners

symptoms.remedy: if the cutter's defender gets screened on the second screen, silence = open shot; must verbally call "switch"
staggered screens are designed to create a catch-and-shoot; the passer must be pressured to disrupt timing
